info.title: Queue Coordinator Service
info.description: Manages clinic queue: check-in, call-next, deprioritize, and real-time WebSocket updates

doctor_id.description: Set at booking time for specific-doctor bookings. For generic queue patients (null at booking), written back when the doctor calls /call-next.

approaching_notified_at.description: Timestamp when the patient was notified that their turn is approaching (≤3 positions ahead). Null until notification fires; prevents re-firing on subsequent queue ticks.

get.summary: List all active queue entries (staff view)
Get queue position for a patient's appointment

409.description: Cannot check in at this status

post.description: Selects the next checked-in patient using time-aware priority: (1) specific bookings for this doctor whose start_time has arrived are called first, (2) generic queue patients fill gaps between due slots, (3) specific bookings not yet due are called as a fallback if nothing else is ready. For generic patients, doctor_id is written back to their queue entry at this point. On success, publishes a `queue.called` event to RabbitMQ, which triggers an SMS notification to the patient.

doctor_id.description: Doctor's BetterAuth nanoid. When provided, prioritises that doctor's specific bookings over generic queue patients.

404.description: Appointment not found or cannot be completed
Appointment not found or already resolved

post.summary: Move patient to back of queue and set status to checked_in (late arrival penalty)

get.summary: Get currently called patient for a doctor

post.summary: Reset queue at start of day

get.summary: Patient WebSocket — subscribe to queue position updates
get.description:
**WebSocket endpoint** — connect with `ws://` or `wss://`, not HTTP.

Send an HTTP Upgrade request to this path. After upgrade, the server immediately sends the current queue entry as JSON and then pushes updates whenever the patient's position or status changes.

**Query parameters (required):**
- `appointment_id` — the appointment to track
- `token` — a valid JWT (Bearer token from `/api/auth/token`)

The server verifies that the JWT `sub` matches the `patient_id` of the appointment before admitting the connection.

**Messages received (JSON):**
```json
{
  "appointment_id": "...",
  "queue_number": 5,
  "position": 2,
  "status": "waiting",
  "estimated_time": "2026-03-29T10:30:00Z"
}
```

Close codes: `1008` = missing params / invalid token / forbidden, `1011` = internal error.

403.description: Token subject does not own this appointment

get.summary: Staff WebSocket — subscribe to full queue snapshot and live updates
get.description:
**WebSocket endpoint** — connect with `ws://` or `wss://`, not HTTP.

For staff and doctors only. On connect, immediately sends a full snapshot of all active queue entries. Then pushes live updates whenever any entry changes.

**Query parameters (required):**
- `token` — a valid JWT. The token's role must be `staff` or `doctor`.

**Messages received (JSON):**

Initial snapshot:
```json
{ "type": "snapshot", "entries": [ ...QueueEntry ] }
```

Live update (single entry changed):
```json
{ "type": "update", "entry": { ...QueueEntry } }
```

Close codes: `1008` = missing token / insufficient role, `1011` = internal error.

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: JWT for a staff or doctor user (without 'Bearer ' prefix)

403.description: User does not have staff or doctor role
